plan: We will trim SPY to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
All leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) will be sold to zero for capital preservation.
QQQI and QQQ will be increased to favor income and broad index exposure.
CRWD will be trimmed to reduce single name concentration.
This plan aims for capital preservation by de-risking significantly.
A substantial cash buffer will be maintained.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and low drawdown. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but potential upside is also limited.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to meet the concentration mandate.
SOXL will be sold as it is below its stop and to reduce leverage.
SPAL will be trimmed to maintain a small, compliant leveraged ETF exposure.
CRWD will be trimmed to reduce single name concentration.
AMD will be added as a strong scanner setup to blend quality tech.
QQQI will be slightly increased for balanced income exposure.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, aiming for risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index, quality tech, and a small amount of leveraged exposure. Expect moderate drawdowns.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to meet the concentration mandate.
SOXL will be sold as it is below its stop, reallocating capital.
SMH will be trimmed due to its downtrend status.
AMD will be bought aggressively as a top-scoring scanner setup.
CRWD will be added to press a working, high-P&L name.
SPAL will be increased to leverage high-conviction plays within limits.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth plan targets around 4% per month, stretching for growth by leaning into strong setups and leverage. This approach amplifies both upside and potential drawdown. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, and strict stop discipline is critical to manage the higher risk of rapid losses.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to reduce concentration and allow for more income focus.
All leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) will be sold to zero.
High-beta single name CRWD and sector ETF SMH will be sold.
QQQI will be significantly increased to maximize income ETF exposure.
QQQ will be trimmed to reduce growth focus while maintaining some broad index.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield through income ETFs. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff. This is not a savings account.
